mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents using standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead for agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent deployments while ensuring robust security and scalability.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the latest CLI version and makes it executable.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster in your chosen region with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Kagenti application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti application from your Kubernetes cluster. Note that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step upgrades the Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp kagenti-backup:/data /path/to/backup

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup your existing data.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1' to refresh kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with your Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a compatible Helm chart version with your Kubernetes version.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.29

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Kagenti deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
